nodes.context: Yakuza group based in Hiroshima, Japan, formed in 1964 from seven clans.
Largest yakuza syndicate, in conflict with Kyosei-kai since early 1960s.
Anti-Yamaguchi federation formed in 1970, of which Kyosei-kai was a leading member.
Anti-Yamaguchi federation formed in 1989, of which Kyosei-kai was a leading member.
Anti-Yamaguchi federation formed in 1996, including Kyosei-kai and other groups.
Chugoku-based organization, member of Gosha-kai federation.
Chugoku-based organization, member of Gosha-kai federation.

Shikoku-based organization, member of Gosha-kai federation.

edges.evidence_quote: Notably the Kyosei-kai has been in conflict with the Yamaguchi-gumi , the largest yakuza syndicate, since the early 1960s.
edges.context: Kyosei-kai has been in conflict with Yamaguchi-gumi since the early 1960s.

edges.evidence_quote: The Kyosei-kai was a leading member of two anti-Yamaguchi federations, the Kansai Hatsuka-kai (formed in 1970)
edges.context: Kyosei-kai was a leading member of the Kansai Hatsuka-kai federation.

edges.evidence_quote: The Kyosei-kai was a leading member of two anti-Yamaguchi federations, the ... Nishinippon Hatsuka-kai (formed in 1989)
edges.context: Kyosei-kai was a leading member of the Nishinippon Hatsuka-kai federation.

edges.evidence_quote: has formed a new anti-Yamaguchi federation named the Gosha-kai since 1996 with three other Chugoku -based organizations, the Kyodo-kai , the Asano-gumi , the Goda-ikka , and the Shikoku -based Shinwa-kai .
edges.context: Kyosei-kai formed the Gosha-kai federation with other groups in 1996.
